exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

exists some_file.txt
exec sesam add some_file.txt
exists .sesam/objects/some_file.txt.sesam

exec sesam mv some_file.txt moved_file.txt
! exists some_file.txt
! exists .sesam/objects/some_file.txt.sesam
exists moved_file.txt
exists moved_file.txt
exists .sesam/objects/moved_file.txt.sesam

exec cat moved_file.txt
stdout 'Some content'

# The move should appear in the log:
exec sesam log
stdout 'admin renamed some_file.txt to moved_file.txt'
exec sesam verify

# Try the same flow, but this time without the file being revealed:
exec sesam seal --clean
! exists moved_file.txt
exists .sesam/objects/moved_file.txt.sesam

exec sesam mv moved_file.txt double_moved_file.txt
exec sesam verify

-- some_file.txt --
Some content
